compositional_truth_check.reconciliation_notes: The registry lists P1, P2, P3, and P4. Visual evidence confirms only two visible pot structures: one yellow (P1) and one black (P3/P4). P2 is absent or obscured. P3/P4 contains the white rabbit anchor. The soil in all visible containers appears devoid of the expected foliage.

inventory_reconciliation.P1_String_of_Nickels: Systemic Loss: Pot is present but appears empty/barren.
inventory_reconciliation.P2_Mexican_Mint: Systemic Loss: Not visible in the frame.
inventory_reconciliation.P3_Pothos: Systemic Loss: Only the white rabbit anchor remains; no foliage detected.
inventory_reconciliation.P4_Silver_Guest: Systemic Loss: No evidence of the specimen.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil surface, no signs of succulent growth.
plant_audit.P3: White rabbit anchor present; soil surface appears undisturbed but empty.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Appears dark and potentially damp, but lacks organic plant matter.
biome_observations.debris: White particulate matter (likely perlite or mineral substrate) visible on the surface of P3.
biome_observations.anomalies: Total absence of expected botanical specimens across all visible pots.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_current: The sequence shows a static, empty state across all images. No growth, senescence, or movement of plant matter has occurred because the pots appear to be devoid of life throughout the observation period.

visual_health_inference.conclusion: The biome is currently in a state of 'Botanical Failure'. There is no evidence of living plant tissue in the provided images.

anomalies: Complete lack of foliage in pots designated for specific species.
Presence of white rabbit anchor without associated Pothos growth.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a consistent state of emptiness across the observed pots. Despite the registry indicating the presence of four distinct plants, the visual data confirms only the presence of the containers and the white rabbit anchor. The soil surfaces are uniform and show no signs of germination, existing growth, or recent decay, suggesting either a complete systemic loss prior to the start of this sequence or that the pots were never populated as described.
vision_report.confidence: High - The lack of visible plant matter is consistent across all frames.
